# Blog Template

## Purpose

This template defines the required structure for every blog article generated by the Writer. All sections are mandatory.

---

## Template Structure

```markdown
# [TITLE]

**Published by:** Global Health Intelligence Agent  
**Category:** Global Health  
**Reading Time:** ~[N] min read  

---

## Introduction

[2–3 paragraphs introducing the health topic. Set the context.
Explain why this development matters. No statistics yet — save those for Key Insights.]

---

## Background

[Provide necessary background for healthcare professionals who may not be familiar with this topic.
Include: history of the issue, how it developed, which population/region is affected, previous related events if any.]

---

## Key Insights

[This is the core of the article. Present the main findings, data, and discoveries.]

- **Finding 1:** [Explain clearly and factually]
- **Finding 2:** [Include any statistics or study outcomes]
- **Finding 3:** [Include any official guidance or recommendations]

[Add a short paragraph connecting these insights to the broader picture.]

---

## Impact on Healthcare Professionals

[Directly address the reader — what should clinicians, nurses, public health officials, and administrators do with this information?]

- [Actionable implication 1]
- [Actionable implication 2]
- [Actionable implication 3]

[Conclude this section with any recommended monitoring, reporting, or protocol adjustments.]

---

## Conclusion

[Summarize the key takeaway in 2–3 sentences. Restate why this matters for global health.
End with a forward-looking statement about monitoring, research, or next steps.]

---

**Sources:**  
1. [Source Name](URL)  
2. [Source Name](URL)  
```

---

## Rules

- Every section heading must appear exactly as shown.
- Do not combine or skip sections.
- The `Sources` section must list at least 2 references.
- Word count: 700–1200 words total across all sections.
- Tone: professional, factual, direct. Avoid passive voice where possible.
